title: Placental Disorders
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Placental Disorders
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Placental Disorders
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Placental Disorders** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Placental Disorders. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Placental Disorders, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Placental Disorders.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Placental Disorders
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: ACOG Practice Bulletin: Placenta Accreta Spectrum

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Placental Disorders?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Placental Disorders, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Placental Disorders?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Placental Disorders, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Placental Disorders requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Placental Disorders requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: Clinical diagnosis primarily; US has limited sensitivity (~25-50%); MRI if stable and indeterminate
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Retroplacental collection (hyper→hypoechoic over time), placental thickening
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Severe = emergent delivery; mild with preterm = expectant management with monitoring
